name: Monday Night Snack Cake or Howard Cosell Beer
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice and chop the ham, onions, bacon, brick cheese, and flour first.
2. In a large mixing bowl, add the eggs and salt; beat well.
3. Gradually add the flour and stir in the rest of the chopped ingredients.
4. Pour the mixture into a greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
5. Bake at 325°F for 1½ hours.
6. Do not pour off the foamy grease.
7. Let stand for 25 minutes before removing from the pan.
8. The cake may be sliced and served cold or cubed and picked for hors d'oeuvres while watching football.
This recipe is ideal as a snack to serve when guests are watching instant replays and have no time between games to sit down to eat a hot snack. Half the recipe makes a small Bundt pan size snack, serving 10 generously and 20 as hors d'oeuvres.
